commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is indicated; maintainers should review CI and explicitly accept the Codex auth-profile visibility boundary before merge.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain defect was found; the diff is an auth-sensitive in-memory store-routing change with no new dependency, workflow, or external execution source. commands.review_summary:
The PR routes a full tool auth profile store into Codex dynamic/plugin tool construction while keeping non-Codex plugin harness transport auth scoped, and adds regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main scopes Codex's authProfileStore before dynamic tool construction while plugin tool availability depends on that store, and the PR body provides a current-main negative control plus patched terminal proof.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; maintainers should review CI and explicitly accept the Codex auth-profile visibility boundary before merge.

target.title: fix(codex): preserve plugin tool auth profiles
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes Codex/GPT-5.5 runs omitting xAI OAuth-backed tools such as `x_search` when Codex scoped its model-transport auth store to OpenAI/Codex credentials.
- Keeps the full auth profile store available to Codex tool construction, including installed external Codex harness packages that only understand the existing `authProfileStore` field.
- Keeps non-Codex plugin-owned harnesses on the existing scoped transport auth behavior, and also threads an explicit `toolAuthProfileStore` for newer Codex harness code.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex/GPT-5.5 sessions should expose enabled xAI plugin tools when xAI SuperGrok/OAuth auth exists, instead of falling back to only generic `web_search`.

Real environment tested: Linux OpenClaw source checkout, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.1.0, Codex harness, GPT-5.5, existing xAI OAuth profile, no `XAI_API_KEY`; foreground gateway with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1` so channel delivery did not affect tool exposure.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
# Current main negative control at df8505b09d
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18899 OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_PAYLOAD=tools OPENCLAW_DEBUG_CODE_MODE=1 \
  pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 18899 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18899 pnpm openclaw agent --agent main \
  --session-id codex-main-xsearch-probe-20260518-2354b \
  --model openai/gpt-5.5 --timeout 180 --json \
  --message 'Controlled probe from current main source. Please use the x_search tool exactly once...'

# Patched PR compatibility proof at ebd2c00daf with installed external Codex package active
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18902 OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_PAYLOAD=tools OPENCLAW_DEBUG_CODE_MODE=1 \
  pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 18902 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=18902 pnpm openclaw agent --agent main \
  --session-id codex-pr-compat-xsearch-probe-20260519-0011 \
  --model openai/gpt-5.5 --timeout 180 --json \
  --message 'Controlled compatibility probe from patched PR worktree with installed external Codex package active. Please use the x_search tool exactly once...'
```

Evidence after fix: Main negative control returned `agentMeta.provider=openai-codex`, `agentMeta.agentHarnessId=codex`, and the tool list contained `web_search` but no `x_search`; final text was `` `x_search` was not available here, so I could not call it.`` Patched PR compatibility proof returned `agentMeta.provider=openai-codex`, `agentMeta.agentHarnessId=codex`, tool entries included `code_execution` and `x_search`, and `toolSummary.tools=["x_search"]` with `toolSummary.calls=1`.

Observed result after fix: The patched Codex/GPT-5.5 run exposed `x_search` and the model called it exactly once. The live xAI call then reported an OAuth refresh failure, which is a credential/runtime auth issue after tool exposure rather than the original missing-tool wiring bug.

What was not tested: A successful xAI network response after refreshing the local OAuth credential, and a full inbound Telegram long-polling round trip. Earlier user testing of the patched gateway over Telegram reported that the previously missing `x_search` path worked.

## Regression tests

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/xai/x-search.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  6 passed (6)
Tests       379 passed (379)
```

Additional check:

```bash
git diff --check
```

Result: passed with no output.

## Security impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new permission; existing enabled xAI tools become visible in affected Codex runs.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. Codex harnesses now receive the full attempt auth store through the existing harness param so installed Codex packages can construct plugin tools with provider auth. Newer Codex code also receives `toolAuthProfileStore` explicitly.
- New/changed network calls? No new endpoint.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, enabled xAI plugin tools can appear where they were previously hidden.
- Risk and mitigation: This broader store is Codex-only because Codex builds OpenClaw tools inside its harness; non-Codex plugin-owned harnesses keep the scoped store. Model auth selection still uses the resolved model auth profile/API key path, and existing plugin/tool policy gates still apply.

## Compatibility

Backward compatible. The fix intentionally supports installed external Codex packages that do not yet know about `toolAuthProfileStore` by keeping tool auth available on `authProfileStore` for the Codex harness.
target.author: rubencu
